pr.title: Fix prev_ch and invalid_prev_ch types in tev
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Corrected TEV color-channel cache globals in src/sysdolphin/baselib/tev.c so prev_ch and invalid_prev_ch are typed as HSD_Chan[4] instead of raw int[48] initializers. The PR body states the old integer-array representation would make matching HSD_SetupChannel impossible, so this was a preparatory type/struct fix for future decomp work. It also changed prev_amb_invalid and prev_mat_invalid from anonymous two-int structs to int[2] arrays and updated invalidation assignments accordingly, plus renamed local variables in HSD_SetupChannelAll to clearer names without changing behavior.
what_changed: Replaced static int prev_ch[48] with static HSD_Chan prev_ch[4], preserving the same apparent field data through structured HSD_Chan initializers.
Replaced static int invalid_prev_ch[48] with static HSD_Chan invalid_prev_ch[4], matching the corrected type of prev_ch and keeping memcpy(&prev_ch, &invalid_prev_ch, sizeof(prev_ch)) coherent.
Changed prev_amb_invalid and prev_mat_invalid from anonymous structs with unk0 and unk4 fields into int[2] arrays.
Updated _HSD_StateInvalidateColorChannel to set prev_amb_invalid[0], prev_amb_invalid[1], prev_mat_invalid[0], and prev_mat_invalid[1] instead of .unk0/.unk4.
Added local declarations idx, chan, and no inside the currently NOT_IMPLEMENTED HSD_SetupChannel stub, likely preparing for future matching work.
Renamed HSD_SetupChannelAll locals from var_r31/temp_r3 to num/temp while preserving the loop logic that computes the maximum HSD_Channel2Num result before calling HSD_StateSetNumChans.
smart_moves: Converted flattened integer data into real HSD_Chan arrays before attempting HSD_SetupChannel, avoiding a misleading data layout that the PR author said made matching impossible.
Kept the invalid-state reset path simple by making invalid_prev_ch the same HSD_Chan[4] type as prev_ch, allowing the existing memcpy reset pattern to remain type-consistent.
Preserved known initializer values while expressing them through the actual struct shape, improving semantic clarity without broad behavioral changes.
Used int[2] for prev_amb_invalid and prev_mat_invalid where the code only indexes two invalid flags, avoiding fake anonymous field names unk0 and unk4.
decomp_lessons: Do not leave statically initialized struct arrays as flattened int arrays when upcoming functions take or compare the real struct type; such placeholders can block matching by obscuring loads, offsets, and sizeof behavior.
For cache/invalid sentinel data, make the valid and invalid copies share the same concrete type so memcpy and sizeof relationships reflect the original code.
Anonymous structs with unk0/unk4 fields are not always the best representation for two adjacent flags; if surrounding code behaves like an array, an int[2] can be a better decomp model.
Small type-only corrections in shared state files can be valuable even before the main function is decompiled, because they reduce future matching work for whoever tackles the function next.
naming_conventions: Use HSD_Chan for channel state records rather than generic int storage when data represents color-channel structs.
Use invalid_prev_ch as the invalid/sentinel counterpart to prev_ch with the same HSD_Chan[4] type.
Prefer descriptive local names such as num and temp over compiler-register-derived placeholders like var_r31 and temp_r3 once behavior is understood.
Use array indexing names prev_amb_invalid[0/1] and prev_mat_invalid[0/1] when the data is modeled as two invalid flags.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Model static data with the real struct type to align field offsets and aggregate size with functions such as HSD_SetupChannel.
When a reset function copies one static object into another via memcpy, use matching source and destination types and sizeof(destination) to document the intended layout.
Keep behavior-preserving local renames separate from type/layout fixes so future matching can focus on actual codegen-relevant differences.
The PR specifically identifies correcting prev_ch and invalid_prev_ch away from integer arrays as necessary preparation for matching HSD_SetupChannel.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that implement or match HSD_SetupChannel after prev_ch and invalid_prev_ch were converted to HSD_Chan arrays.
Check whether prev_amb_invalid and prev_mat_invalid remain int[2] arrays in later baselib/tev.c revisions or are given stronger semantic names.
Search for other flattened static int arrays in sysdolphin baselib that actually represent HSD structs and may block matching.
Compare HSD_Chan struct field order against the prev_ch and invalid_prev_ch initializers to confirm all fields are named and understood.
